perturbation.original_text: FCE will promptly disclose to ExxonMobil, in written or other tangible form, any and all Program Information including any Program Inventions. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, FCE agrees to hold Program Information in confidence, and not to disclose or make it available to any third party without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty (20) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, FCE agrees to use and practice Program Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 7 (License to Program Results).
perturbation.changed_text: FCE will promptly disclose to ExxonMobil, in written or other tangible form, any and all Program Information including any Program Inventions. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, FCE agrees to hold Program Information in confidence, and not to disclose or make it available to any third party without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending five (5) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, FCE agrees to use and practice Program Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 7 (License to Program Results).
perturbation.explanation: The change reduces the confidentiality period for Program Information from twenty years to five years. This directly contradicts Article 4.02, which states a twenty-year confidentiality period for Background Information. This creates an ambiguity regarding the actual duration of confidentiality, since program information and background information may overlap, or program information can make use of the background information and create an in-text contradiction as the background information are suppose to stay confidential for a longer period of time.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE 4 - DISCLOSURE, CONFIDENTIALITY AND RESTRICTED USE - Paragraph 4.01
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE 4 - DISCLOSURE, CONFIDENTIALITY AND RESTRICTED USE - Paragraph 4.02
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will make available its Background Information to the other Party that it believes will be useful in carrying out work under the Program. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, each Party agrees to hold the Background Information it receives from the other Party in confidence, and to not disclose or make available the other Party's Background Information to any third party without the express prior written consent of the other Party, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty (20) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of the other Party, each Party agrees to use and practice the other Party's Background Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 8 (License to Background Information and Patents).
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period for 'Program Information' to five years, while the 'Contradicted Text' specifies a twenty-year confidentiality period for 'Background Information'. Since program information can make use of background information, this creates a direct conflict, as the background information should remain confidential for a longer period than the program information under the changed text. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, making the contract inconsistent and unclear.
